title: Persona-metrics governance reversal undeclared
body: The repo's existing persona-metrics spec explicitly said 'the pipeline never silently drops a persona.' This token-economics spec reverses that before proving measurement quality, without acknowledging it as a governance change.

title: Persona authors have no recourse loop when pruned
body: Spec auto-prunes personas via ranking but there is no path for a persona author to be notified their persona was dropped, understand why, or appeal/iterate. e7 only resets the window on mtime change — that's a quiet incentive to edit-to-reset, not a feedback loop.

title: Non-TTY tier prompt hangs autorun — must never prompt headless
body: Tier-detection prompt UX not testable — interactive prompt inside a non-interactive gate dispatch will hang headless /autorun. Define behavior when stdin is not a TTY (must fall back to default pro, not block).

title: value_per_cost_score formula is broken (gameable, divide-by-zero, severity-blind)
body: (survival_rate + uniqueness_rate) / tokens rewards cheapness too aggressively and ignores severity. A persona producing one cheap minor unique finding can outrank a more expensive persona catching blockers. Also: total_tokens undefined; divide-by-zero when total_tokens==0.

title: Combined #1+#3 reverses BACKLOG sequencing — split into MVP phases
body: Combining #1 (instrumentation) + #3 (roster scaling) creates a hard coupling that hurts both. Instrumentation alone is shippable and immediately useful. Roster scaling depends on instrumentation having ≥10 runs of clean data — exactly what BACKLOG #1 sequencing note called out.

title: /wrap-insights ranking bare-arg duplicates dashboard tab
body: Listed in UX, absent from acceptance criteria; duplicates what the dashboard tab already does. Cut.

title: Settings example uses JSONC syntax in a JSON file
body: spec.md:103-109 shows comments inside settings/settings.json. JSON does not support comments. Strip them before declaring schema.

title: Window unit ambiguity (45 runs of WHAT)
body: 'Rolling window of last 45 runs' — runs of what? Sessions? Gate invocations? Per-(persona,gate) invocations? runs_in_window: 18 in the example for a single (persona, gate) row implies per-(persona,gate), but e1 ('3+ runs accumulate') and e6 ('mtime > 14 days') use a different cadence.

title: No outcome criterion measuring fix actually helped Pro
body: External feedback source (Pro-friend) is unrepresented in acceptance criteria. The original diagnosis came from outside the project. No A-criterion validates the change actually fixes the rate-limit pain that triggered the spec — A1–A10 are all internal artifact checks.

title: Summary contradicts ceiling defaults (Max ≠ 'full set')
body: Summary line 20 says Max/API get the full set; spec.md:84 sets ceiling_by_tier.max=8, api=8, but the default roster is 28 personas — so Max/API are also getting a 71%-pruned roster.

title: e7 persona-mtime breaks under git checkout — use content hash
body: Window-reset on personas/<name>.md mtime (e7) breaks under git checkout. Mtime resets when you git pull or git checkout to a branch with persona changes — even for personas that didn't actually change. Use git blame / file content hash instead of mtime.

title: Resolver-failure fallback unspecified — gate could hang or dispatch zero personas
body: What happens if persona-rankings.jsonl is malformed (truncated JSONL, unknown gate, missing field)? e6 covers staleness only. Need acceptance: parse failure → resolver falls back to full default roster + prints one-line warning; gate proceeds.

title: Minimum sample rule missing from acceptance criteria
body: A2 has no minimum sample-size guard before a value is trusted for ranking. e1 says '3+ runs' for new personas, but A2 has no equivalent — a persona with 1 run and survival_rate=0.0 could be auto-pruned.

title: Cross-project file concurrency on persona-rankings.jsonl
body: Two gates can run simultaneously across two MonsterFlow projects on the same machine; /wrap-insights in one project rewrites this file while resolve-roster.sh in another reads it. Define: per-project or global? Atomic write + read-tolerance required.

title: Override mechanism is V2 in disguise
body: Pin-in/pin-out + floor + ceiling-by-tier is a config surface that needs documentation, validation, error handling. v1 should ship floor + ceiling only; defer always/never lists to v1.1.

title: Per-plugin rows should be a sister artifact, not mixed into persona-rankings.jsonl
body: A9 says '(or sister artifact)' — pick one. Mixing different keys (persona,gate vs plugin,gate) into one file violates the row-shape contract. Use dashboard/data/plugin-costs.jsonl.

title: Cache file ~/.claude/monsterflow-tier mirrors PEM-incident dotfile pattern
body: Spec writes ~/.claude/monsterflow-tier chmod 600. The precedent of 'single line, chmod 600' mirrors the pattern that caused the 2026-04-20 PEM incident. Specify: cache or config? If config, it should live at ~/.config/monsterflow/tier or settings/settings.local.json.

title: Pro user has no visibility into what got cut at gate time
body: Per-gate stdout lists the kept roster but not the dropped roster — so a Pro user sees '4 personas ran' with no indication that 24 were silently filtered. The friend-on-Pro who diagnosed the cost issue would still not see the size of the cut at gate time.

title: e1/e6 stale-cache fallback re-degrades Pro — defeats core purpose
body: Edge cases e1 and e6 both fall back to 'full roster' — which on Pro is exactly the rate-limit-blowout scenario this spec exists to prevent. Fallback should be tier-aware.

title: A9/A10 plugin-cost criteria reference TBD methodology — pull or downgrade
body: A9/A10 acceptance criteria require per-plugin cost rows and a 25%-threshold recommendation, but Open Question #3 admits the methodology isn't decided. Per-plugin attribution may be calibration-only, not retroactive. Per Feasibility's probe: there is no per-plugin marker in session JSONL.

title: Tie-break / determinism unspecified — A4 untestable
body: Top-N selection rule unspecified — what ordering when two personas tie? A4 ('chosen personas match top-ranked from cache') cannot pass without deterministic tie-break.

title: Tier detection cascade (a)/(c) is fictional — verified empirically
body: I verified locally: `claude config --help` does not expose a `config` subcommand or plan/tier field. The current Claude CLI has `auth`, `agents`, `project`, `plugin`, etc., but no usable `claude config get plan`. The JSONL fallback is also speculative.

title: Per-persona token attribution unproven — spike required
body: Per-persona token attribution depends on a session-JSONL structure that doesn't exist for this pipeline. Subagent usage rows may land in a different JSONL file (separate sessionId). Persona name appears only in free-text description and prompt, not structured. Joinability between parent dispatch and subagent usage is undefined.
